title: Montenegro developer signs grid deal for 385 MW solar plant
content: M Energy d.o.o. has signed a grid-connection agreement with Montenegro's transmission operator for a 385 MW solar project, which would be the largest in a country with around 30 MW of installed PV capacity.

title: 20-month field tests show tracker-based bifacial PV outpeforms fixed tilt counterparts with 13.5% higher ouput in desert enviroments
content: Qatari researchers found that bifacial 2P horizontal single-axis tracking PV systems in desert conditions produced up to 13.5% more electricity than fixed-tilt systems over long-term field testing in Qatar. The study also showed strong seasonal and technology-dependent performance, with tracker advantages varying with irradiance and weather conditions.

title: Europe’s solar and storage development: Selectivity amid rising global supply and returning energy shocks
content: Escalating Middle East tensions and global energy supply risks are accelerating Europe’s shift toward solar and storage, particularly in commercial, industrial, and utility-scale segments where energy security, resilience, and price stability are becoming central investment drivers. At the same time, expanding manufacturing capacity in China and India is redirecting surplus solar and storage supply toward Europe, creating a highly competitive and increasingly selective market where long-term suc

title: Inorganic perovskite solar cell based on tetraphenyl-porphine zinc interlayer achieves 82% fill factor
content: An international research team has proposed a perovskite solar cell architecture incorporating a thin tetraphenyl-porphine zinc interfacial layer to enhance surface potential, passivate defect states, and improve charge transport. The strategy leads to improved device efficiency and operational stability, enabling power conversion efficiencies exceeding 13%.

title: Australian researchers develop vapour-based manufacturing process for lead-free perovskite solar
content: Researchers at the University of Queensland have developed a scalable, lead-free perovskite solar cell manufacturing process that achieves record indoor-light efficiency while avoiding toxic materials and enabling flexible consumer electronics applications.

title: California sets precedent by overriding local blockade for massive solar-plus-storage project
content: California is asserting state authority to revive the $700 million Soda Mountain project, marking a new era where Sacramento can reclaim authority from local counties to force stalled utility-scale solar and storage across the finish line.

content:

title: Farmers Are Choosing Solar to Maintain their Farms. Localities Shouldn’t Take Away That Choice.
content:
America’s family farmers are facing mounting challenges. Input costs like fertilizer and diesel are going up while international markets recede and commodity prices crater. Despite these challenges, American farmers are resilient and resourceful. They make decisions every day that balance risk, reward, and long-term stewardship. They adapt to volatile commodity ... title: Solar inverters can detect cyberattacks but no one sees the signal
content: A King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST) researcher whose lab has demonstrated up to 100% accuracy using a single hardware counter tells pv magazine that firmware-level detection of inverter attacks is technically viable – but today’s communication standards do not transmit the firmware‑integrity signal to operators.

title: US utility signs 4 GW solar, storage buildout deal
content: Salt River Project and NextEra Energy Resources have signed a power purchase agreement for 3,000 MW of solar and 1,000 MW of battery storage to be built in Arizona through 2027.

title: Chinese PV Industry Brief: Longi, Trina Solar report losses
content: Longi says its 2025 revenue and losses declined year on year, while Trina Solar reports deeper full-year losses and margin pressure across its core module business.

title: Submarine link to connect island solar to South Korea’s grid
content: Taihan Cable & Solution says it has secured a contract to supply and install 154 kV submarine cables linking island‑based PV arrays and floating solar sites to the South Korean grid, in its first fully integrated project with its marine installation subsidiary.

title: People, purpose and systems thinking: The leadership layer of the energy transition
content: Women in Solar+ Europe highlights this week the insights shared by industry leaders during the panel “Impact Leadership in Solar & Storage: securing the energy transition through people, purpose and systems thinking,” organised by the leadership ecosystem Solar+ Leaders at Solar & Storage Live London. Bringing together voices from across the value chain, the discussion explored how leadership is evolving in response to growing system complexity, skills gaps, and the need for greater alig

title: Myanmar commissions two 40 MW solar plants in Mandalay region
content: The government of Myanmar has opened two 40 MW solar power plants in Mandalay region, adding approximately 202 million kWh of annual generation capacity as the country faces ongoing grid constraints and power rationing.

title: April ENSO shift lifts northern coastal irradiance while suppressing Brazil and northern Argentina
content: In a new weekly update for pv magazine, Solcast, a DNV company, reports that April 2026 brought mixed solar irradiance conditions across South America, with above-average GHI along Colombia’s Pacific and Caribbean coasts and parts of southern Argentina, while eastern and northern temperate regions — including Buenos Aires to Bolivia — saw reduced irradiance due to persistent cloud cover and wetter-than-average conditions.

title: TotalEnergies, Nextnorth close financing for 440 MW Philippines solar
content: TotalEnergies and Philippines-based developer Nextnorth have started construction of a 440 MWp solar plant in Isabela province after reaching financial close on $300 million of international bank financing.

title: Bangladesh’s PV capacity to reach 8.5 GW by 2035, says GlobalData
content: Bangladesh's solar capacity is forecast to increase more than sixfold over the next decade, driven by a shift from off-grid rural deployment toward grid-connected and distributed generation, according to a new GlobalData report.

title: Illinois Shines celebrates Walldog Solar, one of the program’s 171 approved agrivoltaics projects
content: As the Illinois Shines program approaches 4 GW of approved capacity, it’s offering a closer look at one special traditional community solar installation with agrivoltaic and pollinator-friendly attributes.

title: Western grid enters new era as CAISO launches Extended Day-Ahead Market
content: The May 1 go-live of the Extended Day-Ahead Market (EDAM) marks the first time Western utilities can coordinate large-scale solar and storage resources 24 hours in advance, a move projected to significantly slash curtailment and boost regional reliability.

title: First Solar reaffirms 2026 guidance as CuRe launch and record India sales drive Q1 margin expansion
content: Thin-film manufacturer First Solar delivered a robust start to 2026, posting record first-quarter revenue of $1.04 billion and maintaining its full-year outlook despite signaling a seasonal dip in second-quarter profitability.

title: SRP and NextEra Energy Resources partner on massive solar and storage expansion
content: Salt River Project and NextEra Energy Resources have signed a power purchase agreement for 3,000 MW of solar and 1,000 MW of battery storage to be built in Arizona through 2027.

title: This solar farm lets cattle roam under moving panels
content: Silicon Ranch has brought something online commercially that you don’t see every day: a utility-scale solar farm where cattle graze under moving panels.

title: ENGWE anniversary sale $1,048 savings, Lectric e-bikes get up to $793 savings, Segway Max G30P e-scooter $500 low, more
content: We’re closing out this week’s Green Deals with an EV-packed edition, starting with ENGWE’s 12th Anniversary Sale that is offering up to $1,048 savings and bundled gear on three e-bikes, one of which is the

title: Non-compliance with a looming NERC deadline could cost clean energy owners and operators, big time
content: Owners of inverter-based resources (IBRs) have been required to register with NERC by May 15, 2026, or face steep penalties.

title: How VPPs could improve grid reliability and save utilities 40% over gas peaker plants
content: A new report outlines how virtual power plants can free up capacity on the existing grid while highlighting how VPPs can also help meet new load growth driven by data centers.

title: Are Trump’s efforts against clean energy in vain? – This Week in Cleantech
content: On this episode of the This Week in Cleantech podcast, Oliver Milman from The Guardian discusses how the clean energy industry is pushing forward despite Trump's aggressive efforts to suppress it.

title: Project Nexus solar canal demo completes construction in California
content:
Project Nexus, the unique solar system being installed over an irrigation canal in California, has completed construction. Launched in 2022, with construction beginning in 2024, Project Nexus is a pilot study designed to test how solar over canals can generate distributed renewable electricity while saving water and conserving land by utilizing existing canal infrastructure. The…

title: Installation starts on 140-MW solar project in West Texas
content:
Construction has begun on Iron Spur Solar, a 140-MWDC utility-scale solar projet in Snyder, Texas. Iron Spur is being developed by utility-scale solar company Levona Renewables, with financial backing from Energea, a renewable energy developer, operator and investor. The Iron Spur project will use single-axis trackers and is expected to generate approximately 317 GWh of…

title: Solar Without Sacrificing Farmland: Why Parking Lots May Become the Next Layer of Energy Infrastructure
content:
By Terence Parker, Compliance & Technical Training Specialist America is entering what could be the largest expansion of electrical infrastructure since the modern grid was built. Rapid growth in electric vehicles, heat pumps, AI-driven data centers, and more is expected to dramatically increase electricity demand, potentially requiring two to four times the power the grid…

title: 1GWh+ of BESS progressed across EU by re:cap, Flower, Goldbeck, Tavion, LONGi, Teos and SPP
content: Large-scale BESS projects have been progressed to construction, financing or operations across the EU this week, by project owners, investors and EPC firms re:cap, LONGi, Teos, Flower, Goldbeck Solar, SPP and Tavion.
